title: Listening - Part 2
description: You will hear a musician called Ryan talking about a residential music recording camp. For questions 9–18, complete the sentences with a word or short phrase.
instructions: You will hear a musician called Ryan talking about a residential music recording camp. For questions 9–18, complete the sentences with a word or short phrase.

extracts.context: A musician called Ryan talks about a residential music recording camp.
extracts.audio_script: Hello. I’m Ryan, and I coordinate Woodland Sounds, a residential music camp for students aged sixteen to nineteen. Participants spend five days writing, playing and recording music in a quiet country setting. The camp is not in a purpose-built school. Our main accommodation and practice rooms were once part of a [9]country hotel[/9], which closed before being taken over by an arts charity. The bedrooms have been modernised, while the former dining room is now large enough for group rehearsals and evening performances.||Students can apply whether they sing, play an instrument or are more interested in technical work. In previous summers, pop songs were requested most often. This year, however, more applicants have said they want to work on [10]film music[/10], creating short pieces to accompany scenes and images. On arrival day, students meet their groups and tour the site. Before any recording takes place, all participants attend a session on [11]hearing protection[/11], because listening at high volume through speakers or headphones for long periods can cause harm. We want good habits from the start.||The camp supplies microphones, keyboards, amplifiers and recording equipment. Students may bring their own instruments, clearly labelled, and should definitely bring [12]headphones[/12] that they already find comfortable. Their first practical task is not recording a complete track. Working in small groups, students begin by producing a short [13]soundtrack[/13] for a silent video clip, combining music and everyday sounds. This activity means students with very different abilities can contribute immediately and learn to listen to one another.||Recording takes place in several rooms, but our best-equipped space is the new [14]studio cabin[/14], a wooden building at the edge of the grounds with soundproofing and computers. Groups take turns using it. During the week, professional guests visit. We invited a concert singer last year, but the most requested expert this time is a [15]sound engineer[/15], who will explain how recordings are balanced and improved after musicians have played. Students interested in technical careers are especially keen on this session. There is free time each afternoon, including walks and table tennis.||At the end of camp, groups share their completed work at an informal [16]listening party[/16], where nobody is judged and parents can hear the tracks. Each student receives a digital [17]album download[/17] containing camp recordings once permission forms are complete. The charity wants to offer a small number of places to students who cannot afford fees. Therefore, income from our winter concert will support a new [18]scholarship fund[/18]. Applications for this summer require a short statement, not an audition recording, and forms should be submitted by the end of next month.

dialogue.speaker: ryan

Participants spend five days writing, playing and recording music in a quiet country setting. The camp is not in a purpose-built school. In previous summers, pop songs were requested most often. This year, however, more applicants have said they want to work on film music, creating short pieces to accompany scenes and images. We invited a concert singer last year, but the most requested expert this time is a sound engineer, who will explain how recordings are balanced and improved after musicians have played. There is free time each afternoon, including walks and table tennis.||At the end of camp, groups share their completed work at an informal listening party, where nobody is judged and parents can hear the tracks. Each student receives a digital album download containing camp recordings once permission forms are complete. The charity wants to offer a small number of places to students who cannot afford fees.
